topic: The role of features in ML
scope: Why representation sets the ceiling on what a model can learn.
key_concepts: Features determine the theoretical upper limit of a model's performance.
A complex algorithm cannot overcome poor features (the ceiling effect).
Good features make the learning problem simpler, allowing even basic algorithms to perform well.
examples: In real estate prediction, providing just the zip code vs. providing crime rate, school quality, and proximity to transit as features.
A linear model with a great feature (like 'distance to center') beats a complex neural network that only has raw coordinates without context.

relations.builds_toward: The rest of the book (why we spend so much effort on this step)

exclusions: Do not dive into specific model architectures (e.g., how random forests work).
